name: framework-migration-deps-upgrade
description: You are a dependency management expert specializing in safe, incremental upgrades of project dependencies. Plan and execute dependency updates with minimal risk, proper testing, and clear migration pa
capabilities: Analyze current dependencies and identify available updates
Categorize updates by semver (major, minor, patch) with risk assessment
Detect breaking changes between package versions
Generate step-by-step migration guides for major upgrades
Build compatibility matrices for multi-dependency upgrades
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill provides dependency upgrade guidance with example code in markdown. No actual command execution, cryptographic code, or malicious patterns exist. The detected patterns are illustrative code examples within documentation.
valueStatement: This skill helps developers plan and execute dependency upgrades with minimal risk, providing risk assessment, migration guides, and automated testing strategies.
limitations: Does not execute actual package manager commands
Requires user to run tests and verify changes manually
Cannot guarantee compatibility with custom configurations
May not detect all breaking changes in edge cases

useCases.title: Legacy Project Modernization
useCases.description: Safely upgrade a project with many outdated dependencies, prioritizing security updates and managing breaking changes systematically.

useCases.title: Framework Version Migration
useCases.description: Plan a major framework upgrade (e.g., React 16 to 18) with detailed migration steps and rollback procedures.

useCases.title: Security Patch Management
useCases.description: Identify and apply security-critical dependency updates while ensuring stability across the application.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Dependency Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze our project dependencies and create an upgrade plan. List all outdated packages with their current and latest versions, categorize by update risk level, and recommend the safest order to upgrade.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new dependency upgrade project

promptTemplates.title: Major Version Migration
promptTemplates.prompt: We need to upgrade [framework/package] from version X to version Y. This is a major version change. Create a detailed migration guide including breaking changes, code modifications needed, and testing strategy.

promptTemplates.title: Batch Update with Rollback
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a comprehensive plan to update multiple dependencies at once. Include rollback procedures for each package, test strategies to verify compatibility, and a timeline for incremental testing.

promptTemplates.title: Security Update Only
promptTemplates.prompt: Focus only on security-related dependency updates. Identify packages with known vulnerabilities, show the security patches included in newer versions, and create a minimal-risk update plan.

bestPractices: Always create a rollback point (git tag, backup files) before upgrading dependencies
Upgrade incrementally: patch first, then minor, then major versions
Run automated tests after each dependency update to verify compatibility
Review changelogs and breaking changes before major version upgrades
antiPatterns: Upgrading all dependencies at once without testing intermediate states
Skipping patch and minor updates, only addressing major version changes
Ignoring deprecation warnings that precede breaking changes
Not reviewing changelogs before upgrading to new major versions

faq.question: Does this skill actually update my dependencies?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance, plans, and best practices. You must execute package manager commands (npm, pip, etc.) yourself after reviewing the recommendations.

faq.question: How does this skill assess risk?
faq.answer: It categorizes updates by semver (major, minor, patch) and analyzes changelogs for breaking changes, deprecation warnings, and known issues.

faq.question: Can I use this for any package manager?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill provides general guidance applicable to npm, pip, yarn, and other package managers. It includes examples for multiple ecosystems.

faq.question: What if a dependency upgrade breaks my code?
faq.answer: The skill creates rollback procedures including git tags and file backups. Always test in a staging environment before production deployments.

faq.question: How often should I run dependency upgrades?
faq.answer: Check for updates monthly. Prioritize security patches immediately. Plan major version upgrades during lower-activity periods with adequate testing time.

faq.question: Does this work with monorepos?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can analyze multiple package.json files and create coordinated upgrade plans across monorepo packages.
